steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures around the blank
steps.content: The left side of the blank is a noun phrase: “tales of whose exploits, told and retold over time.” The right side is another noun phrase: “myths.” The blank requires a verb form that connects these two noun phrases into a complete clause.

steps.title: Select the past tense verb form that completes the clause
steps.content:
”eventually became” supplies the simple past tense verb that creates the independent clause “tales…eventually became myths,” where “tales” is the subject and “myths” is the object, matching the past tense of the main clause “were based.” Options A, C, and D use participial or infinitive forms that cannot function as the main verb in this clause structure.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
